Project 1

Author

Carson Neubert, Ben Parker, Minh Ho-Hoang

Load Libraries

library(tidyverse)
library(gt)
library(httr)
library(tidyjson)
library(jsonlite)
library(rvest)
library(here)

Week 1

Loading in the Data

# reading in from github
cases_month <- readr::read_csv('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2025/2025-06-24/cases_month.csv')
cases_year <- readr::read_csv('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2025/2025-06-24/cases_year.csv')

Data Description

The following data concerns measles and rubella cases. The data was from the World Health Organization (WHO) between 2012 and June 6, 2025. This includes monthly and yearly data across multiple countries grouped into WHO regions, where each row corresponds to a specific country within a region. The data is provisional and is early case reports by WHO member countries collected using each countries national surveillance systems. The data contains several types of case confirmations (suspected, clinical, epidemiologically linked, and lab confirmed) as well as population count data in the yearly data set. The data was collected to monitor measles and rubella cases worldwide in order to support public health decision making and vaccination strategies.

  • Data source

Cleaning Performed

The given CSVs are cleaned mostly through formatting and data type conversions. All variable names have been standardized into all lowercase with underscores. In the cases_month dataset, numeric variables were converted from a string to have a proper numeric type. In cases_year dataset, the first row was removed because it contained a duplicate header. Column names were also changed for simplicity (iso_country_code -> iso3 for example). Additionally, the dataset contains derived variables such as total measles and rubella cases and incidence rates per 100000 people.

Research Questions

To be answered by the data:

  • How does the volume of measles cases change over time?

  • Which areas of the world contribute the most to measles cases?

To be answered using supplemental data:

  • How does vaccination rates affect measles cases by country? (using vaccination data sets likely from WHO)

  • Are measles case rates correlated with GPD and development level of a country? (get GDP and country data from somewhere like gapminder)

Sketches of Possible Plots

Week 2

Unmodified data summaries

Summary 1
- Using cases_month, across all years is there monthly variation in mean lab confirmed measels/rubella cases?

  • Use of unmodified column, custom function
monthly_sums <- function(df, group) {
  if (is.data.frame(df)) {
    df |>
      group_by({{group}}) |>
      summarize(
                `Mean Measles Cases` = mean(measles_lab_confirmed, na.rm = TRUE),
                `Mean Rubella Cases` = mean(rubella_lab_confirmed, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
      mutate(month_abb = month.abb[as.integer(month)]) |>
      pivot_longer(cols = c(`Mean Measles Cases`, `Mean Rubella Cases`),
                   names_to =  "Statistic",
                   values_to = "value") |>
      select(-month) |>
      pivot_wider(names_from = month_abb,
                  values_from = value) |>
      gt() |>
      tab_style(
        style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
        locations = cells_column_labels()) |> 
      tab_header(
        title = md("Mean Lab Confirmed Measles and Rubella Cases Per Month")) |>
      tab_source_note(
        source_note = md("*Table 1: Mean monthly lab-confirmed measles and rubella cases over the course of the study (2012-2025).*")) |>
      fmt_number(
        columns  = where(is.numeric),
        decimals = 1)
  } else {
  stop('Must input a dataframe')
  }
}

monthly_sums(cases_month, month)
Mean Lab Confirmed Measles and Rubella Cases Per Month
Statistic Jan Feb Mar Apr May Jun Jul Aug Sep Oct Nov Dec
Mean Measles Cases 58.1 63.3 73.8 66.0 59.1 44.3 36.0 29.8 28.4 30.0 32.2 31.3
Mean Rubella Cases 15.3 19.5 26.3 26.2 26.1 19.0 14.4 15.8 20.3 21.0 18.4 15.0
Table 1: Mean monthly lab-confirmed measles and rubella cases over the course of the study (2012-2025).

Summary 2
- Using measels_year, which regions of the world contribute the most to measles cases? (only looking at 2025)

  • Use of custom function, iteration, 1 unmodified column, 1 modified column
region_burden <- function(region_name) {
  cases_year |>
    filter(year == 2025, region == region_name) |>
    mutate(measles_total = as.numeric(measles_total)) |>
    summarize(
      region = region_name,
      total_cases = sum(measles_total, na.rm = TRUE)
    )
}

regions <- unique(cases_year$region)

map(regions, region_burden) |>
  bind_rows(.id="id") |> 
  mutate(pct_global = round(total_cases / sum(total_cases) * 100, 1),
         region = region |>
           recode_values(
                    "EMRO" ~ "Eastern Mediterranean",
                    "AFRO" ~ "Africa",
                    "EURO" ~ "Europe",
                    "SEARO" ~ "South-East Asia",
                    "AMRO" ~ "Americas",
                    "WPRO" ~ "Western Pacific")) |>
  arrange(desc(total_cases)) |>
  select(!id) |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(
    title    = md("**Regional Measles Burden**"),
    subtitle = "2025 Contribution to Global Total"
  ) |>
  cols_label(
    region      = "Region",
    total_cases = "Total Cases",
    pct_global  = "% of Global Total"
  ) |>
  fmt_number(columns = total_cases, decimals = 0) |>
  fmt_number(
    columns  = pct_global,
    decimals = 1,
    pattern  = "{x}%"
    ) |>
  tab_style(
    style     = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) |>
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md("*Table 2: Total measles cases and share of global burden by WHO region, 2025.*")
  )
Regional Measles Burden
2025 Contribution to Global Total
Region Total Cases % of Global Total
Eastern Mediterranean 31,052 34.9%
Africa 18,832 21.2%
Europe 18,551 20.9%
South-East Asia 10,856 12.2%
Americas 5,340 6.0%
Western Pacific 4,222 4.8%
Table 2: Total measles cases and share of global burden by WHO region, 2025.

Summary 3: Average lab quality across each year.

Average lab quality is the proportion of lab confirmed measles cases and total reported measles cases (across all testing types).

years <- sort(unique(cases_year$year))

get_lab_quality <- function(y){
  cases_year |>
    filter(year == y)|>
    mutate(lab_quality = measles_lab_confirmed / measles_total) |>
    summarise(avg_lab_quality = mean(lab_quality, na.rm = TRUE),
              .groups = "drop") |>
    mutate(year = y)
}

map(years, get_lab_quality) |>
  bind_rows() |>
  select(year, avg_lab_quality) |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = md("**Lab Confirmation Quality Over Time**")) |>
  fmt_percent(columns = avg_lab_quality, decimals = 1) |>
  cols_label(
    year = "Year",
    avg_lab_quality = "Average Lab Confirmation Share") |>
  tab_style(style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
            locations = cells_column_labels()) |>
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md("*Table 3: Average lab confirmation share by year (2012–2025)*"))
Lab Confirmation Quality Over Time
Year Average Lab Confirmation Share
2012 63.6%
2013 62.0%
2014 63.6%
2015 64.2%
2016 64.9%
2017 66.5%
2018 64.4%
2019 69.5%
2020 66.5%
2021 60.5%
2022 66.8%
2023 74.6%
2024 75.4%
2025 73.8%
Table 3: Average lab confirmation share by year (2012–2025)

Week 3

Dataset Preparation

Need to do some cleaning of the cases_month meta dataset to better match the data for joining.

# need to do some cleaning for the webscraping process
cases_month2 <- cases_month |>
  mutate(month_abb = recode_values(month, 
                        1 ~ "Jan",
                        2 ~ "Feb",
                        3 ~ "Mar",
                        4 ~ "Apr",
                        5 ~ "May",
                        6 ~ "Jun",
                        7 ~ "Jul",
                        8 ~ "Aug",
                        9 ~ "Sep",
                        10 ~ "Oct",
                        11 ~ "Nov",
                        12 ~ "Dec"),
      country_scrape = str_to_lower(country),
      country_scrape = str_replace_all(country_scrape, " ", "-"))

Web-scraped Data

Source
- WeatherandClimate

  • WeatherAndClimate is an online climate zone and data finder for 80,000+ locations in the world based on Köppen climate classification. The data is updated every 10 minutes and historical data is back to 1980.
  • From the base page, users can click countries to visualize monthly temperatures and precipitation.
  • I will be scraping the table (class = tb6) at the url - ex. https://weatherandclimate.com/american-samoa
  • Countries are lowercase and hyphen separated.
  • Units will be imperial.

Data scrape script

As of week 4, we have stored the output and commented out the scrape. This dramatically reduced render time.

country_vec <- c(unique(cases_month2$country_scrape))

grab_weather <- function(country) {
   base_url <- "https://weatherandclimate.com/"
   climate_url <- str_c(base_url,country) 
   
   result <- tryCatch({
       read_html(climate_url)
     }, error = function(e) {
       message("Failed for ", country, e$message)
       NULL
     })
 
   if (is.null(result)) {
     return(NULL) 
   } else {
     result |>
       html_element(".tb6") |>
       html_table() |>
       pivot_longer(col = Jan:Dec, names_to = "month", values_to = "stat") |> 
       filter(Month %in% c("Average precipitation mm (inches)", "Daily mean °C (°F)")) |>
       select(!Year) |>
       pivot_wider(names_from = "Month", values_from = "stat") |>
       rename(mean_temp = 2,
          mean_precip = 3) |>
       mutate(mean_temp = as.numeric(str_extract(mean_temp, "(?<=\\()[0-9.]+")),
             mean_precip = as.numeric(str_extract(mean_precip, "(?<=\\()[0-9.]+")),
             country = country) #got the regex case from AI looks for values in the ()
 
   }
 }
 
 scraped_weather <- map(.x = country_vec, .f = grab_weather) |>
   bind_rows(.id = "id")
 

# save for loading more than once
# write.csv(scraped_weather, "./site/data/weather-scrape.csv")

Meta Update

Combining the monthly cases data with the scraped data.

scraped_data <- read.csv(here("data", "weather-scrape.csv")) 

cases_month3 <- left_join(cases_month2, scraped_data, by = c("month_abb" = "month",  "country_scrape" = "country" ), 
                          relationship = "many-to-many")

# after the join... 2756 of the 22,780 datapoints don't have temp or precip data
# makes sense 19 of the 173 countries failed in the iteration

Country Demographic Dataset

Loading in the data using API key.

As of week 4, we have stored the output and commented out the api call. This dramatically reduced render time.

country_names <- unique(cases_year$country)

api_key <- "hmz9wf1cpAko2WM4Fz3oPInaZIt44Uf9uh3VFfxD"

base_url <- "https://api.api-ninjas.com/v1/country"

get_country_demo <- function(country_name){
  GET(url = base_url,
      add_headers('X-API-Key' = api_key),
      query = list(name = country_name)) |>
  content("text", encoding = "utf-8") |>
  fromJSON() |>
  as.data.frame()
}

country_demographic <- map_dfr(country_names, get_country_demo)

Descripion: The Country Demographic Datset is taken from API Ninja’s Country API, which provides geographic, demographic, and economic statistics about every country in the world. Each row represents a single country and each column is a statistic about that country, of which there are 187 countries included and over 40 different statistics.

Meta Dataset

Combining the combined scraped data and monthly cases with the country demographic area.

# cleaning script to not need api calls
# country_demographic_clean <- country_demographic |>
#   unnest(currency, names_sep = "_")
# 
# write_csv(country_demographic_clean, "data/country_demographic.csv")

api_load <- read.csv("./data/country_demographic.csv")

meta_data <- left_join(cases_month3,
                       api_load,
                       by = c("country" = "name"),
                       relationship = "many-to-many")

Week 3 Delieverables

Visualization:
Is there a relationship between mean temperature measles cases throughout the year?

meta_data |>
  group_by(month, month_abb) |>
  summarize(
    mean_measles = mean(measles_total, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_temp = mean(mean_temp, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop") |>
  mutate(
    mean_measles = as.numeric(scale(mean_measles)),
    mean_temp = as.numeric(scale(mean_temp)),
    month_abb = factor(month_abb, levels = month.abb)) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(mean_measles, mean_temp),
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "value") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = month_abb, y = value, color = variable, group = variable)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Measles Cases and Temperature by Month",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Standardized Value",
    color = "Variable") +
  theme_minimal()

Is there a relationship between a country’s GDP and Measles cases?

meta_data |>
  group_by(country) |>
  summarize(
    mean_measles = mean(measles_total, na.rm = TRUE),
    gdp_per_capita = mean(gdp_per_capita, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop") |>
  filter(mean_measles != 0 & gdp_per_capita != 0) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = gdp_per_capita, y = mean_measles)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 3) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(
    title = "Country GDP per Capita and Mean Measles Cases",
    x = "GDP per Capita (log scale)",
    y = "Mean Measles Cases (log scale)") +
  theme_minimal()

Week 4

Visualization 1

Using a tile plot and correlation matix,

  • How are climate, natural resources and emissions related to monthly measles/rubella cases?
library(corrr)

make_titles <- function(x){
  # expects a character or factor as an input
  stopifnot(is.character(x) | is.factor(x))
  
  # remove the units at the end of the name
  str_remove(x, pattern = "(g|mm)$") |> 
  # replace all _ with spaces
  str_replace_all(pattern = "_", replacement = " ") |> 
  # remove extra whitespace on left 
  str_trim(side = "both") |> 
  # convert the first letter of each word to upper case
  str_to_title()
}


meta_data |>
  select(month, measles_total, measles_epi_linked, rubella_total, 
         rubella_epi_linked, mean_temp, mean_precip, surface_area,
         forested_area, co2_emissions, threatened_species, internet_users) |>
  correlate(method = "pearson",
            use = "pairwise.complete.obs") |>
  rename(term1 = term) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = -term1, 
               names_to = "term2", 
               values_to = "correlation") |>
  mutate(
    across(.cols = c(term1, term2), 
           .fns = ~ make_titles(.x)
           )
    ) |> 
  ggplot(mapping = aes(x = term1,
                       y = term2, 
                       fill = correlation)) +
  geom_tile() +
  labs(x = "", 
       y = "", 
       title = "Correlation Between Measles/Rubella Cases and Biological Factors", 
       fill = "Correlation"
       ) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "B")

## Load Libraries

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(gt)
library(httr)
library(tidyjson)
library(jsonlite)
library(rvest)
library(here)

```

# Week 1 {#week-1}

## Loading in the Data

```{r}
# reading in from github
cases_month <- readr::read_csv('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2025/2025-06-24/cases_month.csv')
cases_year <- readr::read_csv('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2025/2025-06-24/cases_year.csv')
```

## Data Description

The following data concerns measles and rubella cases. The data was from the World Health Organization (WHO) between 2012 and June 6, 2025. This includes monthly and yearly data across multiple countries grouped into WHO regions, where each row corresponds to a specific country within a region. The data is provisional and is early case reports by WHO member countries collected using each countries national surveillance systems. The data contains several types of case confirmations (suspected, clinical, epidemiologically linked, and lab confirmed) as well as population count data in the yearly data set. The data was collected to monitor measles and rubella cases worldwide in order to support public health decision making and vaccination strategies.

-   [Data source](https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/blob/main/data/2025/2025-06-24/readme.md)

## Cleaning Performed

The given CSVs are cleaned mostly through formatting and data type conversions. All variable names have been standardized into all lowercase with underscores. In the cases_month dataset, numeric variables were converted from a string to have a proper numeric type. In cases_year dataset, the first row was removed because it contained a duplicate header. Column names were also changed for simplicity (iso_country_code -\> iso3 for example). Additionally, the dataset contains derived variables such as total measles and rubella cases and incidence rates per 100000 people.

## Research Questions

**To be answered by the data:**

-   How does the volume of measles cases change over time?

-   Which areas of the world contribute the most to measles cases?

**To be answered using supplemental data:**

-   How does vaccination rates affect measles cases by country? (using vaccination data sets likely from WHO)

-   Are measles case rates correlated with GPD and development level of a country? (get GDP and country data from somewhere like gapminder)

# Week 2 {#week-2}

## Unmodified data summaries

**Summary 1**\
- Using cases_month, across all years is there monthly variation in mean lab confirmed measels/rubella cases?

-   Use of unmodified column, custom function

```{r}
monthly_sums <- function(df, group) {
  if (is.data.frame(df)) {
    df |>
      group_by({{group}}) |>
      summarize(
                `Mean Measles Cases` = mean(measles_lab_confirmed, na.rm = TRUE),
                `Mean Rubella Cases` = mean(rubella_lab_confirmed, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
      mutate(month_abb = month.abb[as.integer(month)]) |>
      pivot_longer(cols = c(`Mean Measles Cases`, `Mean Rubella Cases`),
                   names_to =  "Statistic",
                   values_to = "value") |>
      select(-month) |>
      pivot_wider(names_from = month_abb,
                  values_from = value) |>
      gt() |>
      tab_style(
        style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
        locations = cells_column_labels()) |> 
      tab_header(
        title = md("Mean Lab Confirmed Measles and Rubella Cases Per Month")) |>
      tab_source_note(
        source_note = md("*Table 1: Mean monthly lab-confirmed measles and rubella cases over the course of the study (2012-2025).*")) |>
      fmt_number(
        columns  = where(is.numeric),
        decimals = 1)
  } else {
  stop('Must input a dataframe')
  }
}

monthly_sums(cases_month, month)

```

**Summary 2**\
- Using measels_year, which regions of the world contribute the most to measles cases? (only looking at 2025)

-   Use of custom function, iteration, 1 unmodified column, 1 modified column

```{r}
region_burden <- function(region_name) {
  cases_year |>
    filter(year == 2025, region == region_name) |>
    mutate(measles_total = as.numeric(measles_total)) |>
    summarize(
      region = region_name,
      total_cases = sum(measles_total, na.rm = TRUE)
    )
}

regions <- unique(cases_year$region)

map(regions, region_burden) |>
  bind_rows(.id="id") |> 
  mutate(pct_global = round(total_cases / sum(total_cases) * 100, 1),
         region = region |>
           recode_values(
                    "EMRO" ~ "Eastern Mediterranean",
                    "AFRO" ~ "Africa",
                    "EURO" ~ "Europe",
                    "SEARO" ~ "South-East Asia",
                    "AMRO" ~ "Americas",
                    "WPRO" ~ "Western Pacific")) |>
  arrange(desc(total_cases)) |>
  select(!id) |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(
    title    = md("**Regional Measles Burden**"),
    subtitle = "2025 Contribution to Global Total"
  ) |>
  cols_label(
    region      = "Region",
    total_cases = "Total Cases",
    pct_global  = "% of Global Total"
  ) |>
  fmt_number(columns = total_cases, decimals = 0) |>
  fmt_number(
    columns  = pct_global,
    decimals = 1,
    pattern  = "{x}%"
    ) |>
  tab_style(
    style     = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) |>
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md("*Table 2: Total measles cases and share of global burden by WHO region, 2025.*")
  ) 
```

**Summary 3:** Average lab quality across each year.

Average lab quality is the proportion of lab confirmed measles cases and total reported measles cases (across all testing types).

```{r}
years <- sort(unique(cases_year$year))

get_lab_quality <- function(y){
  cases_year |>
    filter(year == y)|>
    mutate(lab_quality = measles_lab_confirmed / measles_total) |>
    summarise(avg_lab_quality = mean(lab_quality, na.rm = TRUE),
              .groups = "drop") |>
    mutate(year = y)
}

map(years, get_lab_quality) |>
  bind_rows() |>
  select(year, avg_lab_quality) |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = md("**Lab Confirmation Quality Over Time**")) |>
  fmt_percent(columns = avg_lab_quality, decimals = 1) |>
  cols_label(
    year = "Year",
    avg_lab_quality = "Average Lab Confirmation Share") |>
  tab_style(style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
            locations = cells_column_labels()) |>
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md("*Table 3: Average lab confirmation share by year (2012–2025)*"))
```

# Week 3 {#week-3}

## Dataset Preparation

Need to do some cleaning of the cases_month meta dataset to better match the data for joining.

```{r}
# need to do some cleaning for the webscraping process
cases_month2 <- cases_month |>
  mutate(month_abb = recode_values(month, 
                        1 ~ "Jan",
                        2 ~ "Feb",
                        3 ~ "Mar",
                        4 ~ "Apr",
                        5 ~ "May",
                        6 ~ "Jun",
                        7 ~ "Jul",
                        8 ~ "Aug",
                        9 ~ "Sep",
                        10 ~ "Oct",
                        11 ~ "Nov",
                        12 ~ "Dec"),
      country_scrape = str_to_lower(country),
      country_scrape = str_replace_all(country_scrape, " ", "-"))


```

## Web-scraped Data

**Source**\
- [WeatherandClimate](https://weatherandclimate.com/)

-   WeatherAndClimate is an online climate zone and data finder for 80,000+ locations in the world based on Köppen climate classification. The data is updated every 10 minutes and historical data is back to 1980.\
-   From the base page, users can click countries to visualize monthly temperatures and precipitation.\
-   I will be scraping the table (class = tb6) at the url - ex. https://weatherandclimate.com/american-samoa
-   Countries are lowercase and hyphen separated.
-   Units will be imperial.

### Data scrape script

**As of week 4, we have stored the output and commented out the scrape. This
dramatically reduced render time.**   

```{r}
#| eval: false

country_vec <- c(unique(cases_month2$country_scrape))

grab_weather <- function(country) {
   base_url <- "https://weatherandclimate.com/"
   climate_url <- str_c(base_url,country) 
   
   result <- tryCatch({
       read_html(climate_url)
     }, error = function(e) {
       message("Failed for ", country, e$message)
       NULL
     })
 
   if (is.null(result)) {
     return(NULL) 
   } else {
     result |>
       html_element(".tb6") |>
       html_table() |>
       pivot_longer(col = Jan:Dec, names_to = "month", values_to = "stat") |> 
       filter(Month %in% c("Average precipitation mm (inches)", "Daily mean °C (°F)")) |>
       select(!Year) |>
       pivot_wider(names_from = "Month", values_from = "stat") |>
       rename(mean_temp = 2,
          mean_precip = 3) |>
       mutate(mean_temp = as.numeric(str_extract(mean_temp, "(?<=\\()[0-9.]+")),
             mean_precip = as.numeric(str_extract(mean_precip, "(?<=\\()[0-9.]+")),
             country = country) #got the regex case from AI looks for values in the ()
 
   }
 }
 
 scraped_weather <- map(.x = country_vec, .f = grab_weather) |>
   bind_rows(.id = "id")
 

# save for loading more than once
# write.csv(scraped_weather, "./site/data/weather-scrape.csv")





```

## Meta Update

Combining the monthly cases data with the scraped data.

```{r}
scraped_data <- read.csv(here("data", "weather-scrape.csv")) 

cases_month3 <- left_join(cases_month2, scraped_data, by = c("month_abb" = "month",  "country_scrape" = "country" ), 
                          relationship = "many-to-many")

# after the join... 2756 of the 22,780 datapoints don't have temp or precip data
# makes sense 19 of the 173 countries failed in the iteration

```

## Country Demographic Dataset

Loading in the data using API key.

**As of week 4, we have stored the output and commented out the api call. This
dramatically reduced render time.**     


```{r}
#| eval: false

country_names <- unique(cases_year$country)

api_key <- "hmz9wf1cpAko2WM4Fz3oPInaZIt44Uf9uh3VFfxD"

base_url <- "https://api.api-ninjas.com/v1/country"

get_country_demo <- function(country_name){
  GET(url = base_url,
      add_headers('X-API-Key' = api_key),
      query = list(name = country_name)) |>
  content("text", encoding = "utf-8") |>
  fromJSON() |>
  as.data.frame()
}

country_demographic <- map_dfr(country_names, get_country_demo)

```

**Descripion:** The Country Demographic Datset is taken from API Ninja's Country API, which provides geographic, demographic, and economic statistics about every country in the world. Each row represents a single country and each column is a statistic about that country, of which there are 187 countries included and over 40 different statistics.

## Meta Dataset

Combining the combined scraped data and monthly cases with the country demographic area.

```{r}
# cleaning script to not need api calls
# country_demographic_clean <- country_demographic |>
#   unnest(currency, names_sep = "_")
# 
# write_csv(country_demographic_clean, "data/country_demographic.csv")

api_load <- read.csv("./data/country_demographic.csv")

meta_data <- left_join(cases_month3,
                       api_load,
                       by = c("country" = "name"),
                       relationship = "many-to-many")


```

## Week 3 Delieverables

**Visualization:**\
Is there a relationship between mean temperature measles cases throughout the year?

```{r}
meta_data |>
  group_by(month, month_abb) |>
  summarize(
    mean_measles = mean(measles_total, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_temp = mean(mean_temp, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop") |>
  mutate(
    mean_measles = as.numeric(scale(mean_measles)),
    mean_temp = as.numeric(scale(mean_temp)),
    month_abb = factor(month_abb, levels = month.abb)) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(mean_measles, mean_temp),
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "value") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = month_abb, y = value, color = variable, group = variable)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Measles Cases and Temperature by Month",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Standardized Value",
    color = "Variable") +
  theme_minimal()
```

Is there a relationship between a country's GDP and Measles cases?

```{r}
meta_data |>
  group_by(country) |>
  summarize(
    mean_measles = mean(measles_total, na.rm = TRUE),
    gdp_per_capita = mean(gdp_per_capita, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop") |>
  filter(mean_measles != 0 & gdp_per_capita != 0) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = gdp_per_capita, y = mean_measles)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 3) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(
    title = "Country GDP per Capita and Mean Measles Cases",
    x = "GDP per Capita (log scale)",
    y = "Mean Measles Cases (log scale)") +
  theme_minimal()
```

# Week 4 {#week-4}     

## Visualization 1    
Using a tile plot and correlation matix,  

- How are climate, natural resources and emissions related to monthly 
measles/rubella cases?    

```{r}
#| fig-height: 8
#| fig-width: 15

library(corrr)

make_titles <- function(x){
  # expects a character or factor as an input
  stopifnot(is.character(x) | is.factor(x))
  
  # remove the units at the end of the name
  str_remove(x, pattern = "(g|mm)$") |> 
  # replace all _ with spaces
  str_replace_all(pattern = "_", replacement = " ") |> 
  # remove extra whitespace on left 
  str_trim(side = "both") |> 
  # convert the first letter of each word to upper case
  str_to_title()
}


meta_data |>
  select(month, measles_total, measles_epi_linked, rubella_total, 
         rubella_epi_linked, mean_temp, mean_precip, surface_area,
         forested_area, co2_emissions, threatened_species, internet_users) |>
  correlate(method = "pearson",
            use = "pairwise.complete.obs") |>
  rename(term1 = term) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = -term1, 
               names_to = "term2", 
               values_to = "correlation") |>
  mutate(
    across(.cols = c(term1, term2), 
           .fns = ~ make_titles(.x)
           )
    ) |> 
  ggplot(mapping = aes(x = term1,
                       y = term2, 
                       fill = correlation)) +
  geom_tile() +
  labs(x = "", 
       y = "", 
       title = "Correlation Between Measles/Rubella Cases and Biological Factors", 
       fill = "Correlation"
       ) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "B")

```
